does: This paper introduces the task of personalized empathy in long-term human-AI interactions, where empathetic strategies are adapted to individual user characteristics rather than applied uniformly. The authors construct PersonaEmp, a dataset derived from extended user-AI conversations annotated with user histories, persona information, and empathy-seeking queries. They propose PereGRM, a reward modeling framework that combines empathy evaluation structures with dynamic criteria generation to enable fine-grained evaluation of empathetic responses. Experimental evaluation across multiple settings and judge models demonstrates that PereGRM substantially improves performance compared to baseline approaches, validating the approach for enhancing personalized empathetic capabilities in LLMs.
connects: This work builds on recent research demonstrating that LLMs can be trained for empathetic dialogue and response generation, extending beyond generic empathy to account for individual differences. It connects to lines of work on personality-aware dialogue systems and user modeling in conversational AI, which have shown that adapting responses to user characteristics improves interaction quality. The reward modeling approach draws from recent advances in preference-based fine-tuning of language models and reinforcement learning from human feedback. The paper addresses a gap between prior work treating empathy as a monolithic capability and emerging understanding that effective empathy requires tailoring to personality traits and individual context, building on psychological research about individual differences in empathetic needs.
enables: This work opens directions for developing more nuanced empathetic AI systems that recognize users have different emotional needs and preferences. Future work can extend personalized empathy to other dialogue capabilities and long-term relationship building, incorporating richer models of personality evolution and context-dependent empathetic needs. The dynamic reward modeling framework could be adapted for personalizing other dialogue aspects beyond empathy. This foundation supports development of more human-centered AI companions that learn and adapt their emotional support strategies over time, potentially improving therapeutic applications, mental health support systems, and long-term AI assistants that genuinely account for individual human differences rather than applying one-size-fits-all approaches.
